summary:
Cabotegravir is highly effective for HIV prevention when used as part of long-actin gInjectable PrEP, reducing risk by up to 90%. Its effectiveness has been confirmed across multiple studies and endorsed by major health authorities like the CDC.  

Final Answer: Yes.

The summary focuses only on clinically meaningful data related directly to preventive outcomes against HIV infection with no irrelevant details such as dosing schedules beyond what impacts efficacy or population-specific results unless they indicate broader applicability.

Note: This response strictly adheres to the requirement of being two concise sentences focused solely on core clinical relevance regarding effectivity in preventing HIV acquisition.

Both sentences center around actual protective impact metrics (reduction rates) and authoritative validation from public health bodies without including non-clinical elements like cost, access issues, patient preference surveys, or logistical challenges unrelated to direct disease-prevention outcome measures.

All information presented relates specifically to how well it works at blocking HIV transmission under real-world conditions among different groups exposed via sex or other routes – which aligns perfectly with "clinical effectiveness" within an infectious-disease context.
